titleEn: Inside One of America’s Largest Ag Co-Op’s Practical Approach to AI
titleZh: 美国最大农业合作社之一对AI的务实实践：从概念到田间落地
summaryZh: 本文通过GROWMARK农艺总监Brendan Bachman的访谈，揭示了北美最大农业合作社系统之一在规模化应用AI时的实际操作挑战与策略。核心观点是：AI不是颠覆性技术，而是嵌入现有工作流程的实用层，旨在减少摩擦、辅助农艺师更快做出更明智的季节内决策。文章强调了数据基础设施建设、联邦制组织协调、用户信任建立以及衡量成功的关键指标（如用户参与度和绩效差距），为智慧畜牧行业在AI落地方面提供了宝贵的实践参考。
featuredReason: 本文提供了大型农业合作社在AI落地中的真实经验与务实策略，强调AI作为辅助工具而非替代品，对智慧畜牧行业在技术选型、数据整合和用户信任建设方面具有直接借鉴意义。
contentFull: By AgriBusiness Global Share AI has long dominated ag industry headlines — but turning it from concept into day-to-day agronomic practice requires far more than a product launch. In this episode of Ag Tech Talk, FS Agronomy Director Brendan Bachman of GROWMARK breaks down the operational reality behind scaling AI across one of North America’s largest cooperative systems, from building data infrastructure to navigating the complexities of a federated organization. Rather than positioning AI as a disruption, Bachman describes it as a practical layer within existing workflows – one aimed at reducing friction and helping crop specialists make faster, more informed in-season decisions. The discussion highlights the less visible work of adoption: aligning systems, earning grower trust, and driving buy-in across FS locations, while tools like MyFS Agronomy increasingly reshape – not replace – the agronomist’s role in the field. MORE BY AGRIBUSINESS GLOBAL Digital Farming June 2, 2026 Inside India’s Agricultural Labor Gap: How AgTech Is Digitizing the Farm Workforce By AgriBusiness Global|June 2, 2026 Analytics June 1, 2026 Ag Tech Talk: Smarter Data, Stronger Relationships By AgriBusiness Global|June 1, 2026 Imagery May 15, 2026 Ag Tech Talk Podcast: EarthDaily on Satellite Data, Crop Monitoring, and Market Transparency By AgriBusiness Global|May 15, 2026 Podcast Transcript: *This is an edited and partial transcript. Top Articles Read More Read More Read More Read More Read More UC Riverside Develops Modern-Day Robotic Divining Rod AgriBusiness Global: As you evaluate success, what metrics matter most right now, and how do you see AI shaping agronomic decision-making and retailer value over the next several seasons? Brendan Bachman: It’s going to get interesting—and in many ways, it already is. What stands out most is that context and content really matter. AI on the open web is one thing, but AI layered on top of a highly curated, agronomy-specific dataset is where you start to see real value emerge. In terms of measuring success, it comes down to engagement. We’re never going to have 100% adoption, so the key is understanding who is using the tools versus who isn’t—and then digging into what that means. Are we seeing sustained usage growth? Are engaged users making better decisions or seeing stronger outcomes, like higher yield performance compared to non-users? That’s where this gets meaningful. AI isn’t a silver bullet; it’s a synthesis tool. It helps bring together the complexity of agronomic decision-making in a way that supports, rather than replaces, human judgment. Over the next few seasons, the real question is whether we begin to see a performance gap emerge between those who are actively using these tools and those who aren’t. If that happens, it becomes less of a technology story and more of a competitiveness story—for both farmers and retailers. ABG: As AI becomes more embedded in agronomic decision-making, do you expect it to influence how growers purchase or use crop inputs—and could it reshape the role of ag retailers and manufacturers? BB: Yes, I do think AI will ultimately influence purchasing decisions and product use. But it’s important to recognize that AI has been evolving in the background for years—through tools like computer vision and automation. What’s changed is the rise of large language models that make these systems far more accessible and interactive. That accessibility is lowering the barrier to entry across ag tech and beyond. We’re seeing entirely new verticals open up faster than ever before, which will likely accelerate innovation—but also drive consolidation. Fewer players will be able to build and sustain differentiated value at scale. In the end, the organizations that succeed will be the ones that use AI effectively to improve decision-making and customer impact. That will directly influence farmer decisions, product selection, and how value flows through the ag retail system. ABG: Any closing thoughts? BB: This isn’t the end of something—it’s the beginning of a new phase. The pace of change is unlike anything I’ve seen in 20 years. But even as we lean into AI, we still must ground it in strong agronomy and sound decision-making. And we also must be willing to challenge our assumptions. One of the biggest lessons here is the need to forget what we think we already know to see what’s actually possible. 0 1 5 Inside One of America's Largest Ag Co-Op's Practical Approach to AI Subscribe Today For Subscribe AgriBusiness Global, a sister publication to PrecisionAg at Meister Media Worldwide, connects the crop input value chain from raw material manufacturers down to distributors and trading partners around the world. See all author stories here. Topics: featured-home
translationZh:
标题：美国最大农业合作社之一对AI的务实实践
来源：AgriBusiness Global

长期以来，AI一直占据农业行业头条——但将其从概念转化为日常农艺实践，需要的远不止一次产品发布。在本期《农业科技对话》中，GROWMARK的农艺总监Brendan Bachman详细剖析了在北美最大的合作社系统之一中规模化AI背后的运营现实，从构建数据基础设施到应对联邦制组织的复杂性。Bachman并未将AI定位为颠覆性力量，而是将其描述为现有工作流程中的一个实用层——旨在减少摩擦，帮助作物专家在季节内做出更快、更明智的决策。讨论重点突出了采用过程中那些不太显眼的工作：协调系统、赢得种植者信任、推动FS各站点的认同，而MyFS Agronomy等工具正在重塑——而非取代——农艺师在田间的角色。

更多来自AgriBusiness Global的内容：
数字农业，2026年6月2日——印度农业劳动力缺口：农业科技如何数字化农场劳动力
分析，2026年6月1日——农业科技对话：更智能的数据，更牢固的关系
图像，2026年5月15日——农业科技对话播客：EarthDaily谈卫星数据、作物监测与市场透明度

播客文字记录（经编辑和节选）：

AgriBusiness Global：在评估成功时，目前哪些指标最重要？您如何看待AI在未来几个季节中塑造农艺决策和零售商价值？

Brendan Bachman：这将变得非常有趣——在很多方面，它已经如此。最突出的一点是，背景和内容确实很重要。开放网络上的AI是一回事，但叠加在高度精选的、特定于农艺的数据集上的AI，才是真正价值开始显现的地方。

在衡量成功方面，关键在于参与度。我们永远不可能达到100%的采用率，因此关键是了解谁在使用这些工具，谁没有——然后深入挖掘这意味着什么。我们是否看到持续的使用增长？活跃用户是否做出了更好的决策，或者看到了更强的成果，比如与非用户相比更高的产量表现？这就是意义所在。AI不是银弹；它是一种综合工具。它有助于将农艺决策的复杂性整合在一起，以支持而非取代人类判断的方式。

在接下来的几个季节里，真正的问题是，我们是否会开始看到积极使用这些工具的人与不使用的人之间出现绩效差距。如果这种情况发生，那么它就不再是一个技术故事，而是一个竞争力故事——对农民和零售商都是如此。

ABG：随着AI越来越深入地嵌入农艺决策，您是否预计它会如何影响……
author: Matt Hopkins
contentHtml:
FS Agronomy Director Brendan Bachman of GROWMARK breaks down the operational reality behind scaling AI across one of North America’s largest cooperative systems.
The post Inside One of America’s Largest Ag Co-Op’s Practical Approach to AI appeared first on Global Ag Tech Initiative.
